areasOfConcern: 18.2% cited supervisor relationship issues as primary exit reason
Multiple reports of racial inequality concerns
Limited response rate (2.96%) affects statistical significance
positiveFeedback: 72.7% would still recommend Creighton as workplace
Strong university culture and mission alignment
Meaningful work with students highly valued

actionItems.text: Investigate reported improper conduct (3 of 11 respondents)
Implement supervisor training program - cited by 18% as exit reason
Address retention in Medicine (82 exits) and Phoenix (92 exits)
Enhance career development programs - limited advancement cited

summaryText:
With a 2.96% response rate (11 of 372 exits), the exit survey provides valuable but limited insights. 72.7% of respondents would still recommend Creighton as a workplace. Key improvement areas include supervisor relationships, career advancement opportunities, and leadership communication.

Positive aspects include strong university culture, meaningful work with students, and competitive benefits package. Improving exit survey participation should be a priority for more comprehensive retention insights and actionable outcomes.
